1: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;
2: To perceive the words of understanding;
4: To the young man knowledge and discretion.
5: And a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:
6: The words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
7: But But fools despise wisdom and instruction.
8: And forsake not the law of thy mother:
11: Let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:
12: And whole, as those that go down into the pit:
13: We shall fill our houses with spoil:
14: Let us all have one purse:
15: Refrain thy foot from their path:
16: And make haste to shed blood.
17: In the sight of any bird.
18: They lurk privily for their own own lives.
19: Which Which taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
20: She uttereth her voice in the streets:
21: In the city she uttereth her words, saying saying ,
23: I will make known my words unto you.
24: I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;
25: And would none of my reproof:
26: I will mock when your fear cometh;
27: When distress and anguish cometh upon you.
28: They shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
29: And did not choose the fear of the LORD LORD :
31: And be filled with their own devices.
32: And the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.
33: And shall be quiet from fear of evil.
